A: The title that Roger O. Egeberg held is Assistant Secretary for Health, Education, and Welfare. Roger Egeberg held this position during the administration of Lyndon B. However, he held the title that was asked for, Assistant Secretary for Health and Scientific Affairs, during the administration of John F. Kennedy from 1961 to 1965.

Johnson from 1965 to 1969, and under George W. Bush briefly from 2001 to 2005. However, he held the title that was asked for, Assistant Secretary for Health and Scientific Affairs, during the administration of John F. Kennedy from 1961 to 1965. Therefore the answer is: 1961 to 1965.

A: "Barbra Streisand sang the song A Rather Blustery Day. And she also voiced the character of Miss Bianca's Mice companion for her last scene, additionally she was an alternate for Miss Bianca, but also she and animation director, Saul Bass (a technician of films) had both lobbied to voice Miss Bianca; however, in the end she was cast as the voice of Rosie's Muppet companion and had a less but notable role than her song and body double"

Q: Kaiser Ventures corporation was founded by an American industrialist who became known as the father of modern American shipbuilding?
A: "The Kaiser family is associated with both, Rhenish and American industry, as well as the construction of steel ships, including naval vessels, in the United States. Thus, the name of the founder of Kaiser Ventures corporation was William Henry Kaiser II. William Henry Kaiser II's father was Henry J. Kaiser, a Rhenish-born American industrialist. Henry J. Kaiser is considered to have been the father of modern American shipbuilding, as he founded the Kaiser Shipyard. So, the answer"
A: The Kaiser family is associated with both, Rhenish and American industry, as well as the construction of steel ships, including naval vessels, in the United States. Thus, the name of the founder of Kaiser Ventures corporation was William Henry Kaiser II. William Henry Kaiser II's father was Henry J. Kaiser, a Rhenish-born American industrialist. Henry J. Kaiser is considered to have been the father of modern American shipbuilding, as he founded the Kaiser Shipyard. So, the answer

5abbf698554299114383a0b5
Which British first-generation jet-powered medium bomber was used in the South West Pacific theatre of World War II?
A: []
A: Mosquito
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Mosquito .
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Mosquito . In the Mediterranean theatre of the Second World War, the De Havilland Mosquito was mainly used for reconnaissance, ferrying, and day and night bombing raids.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Mosquito . In the Mediterranean theatre of the Second World War, the De Havilland Mosquito was mainly used for reconnaissance, ferrying, and day and night bombing raids. In the South West Pacific theatre of the war, the Mosquito was the only Allied aircraft capable of high-altitude bombing, and was consequently, used extensively in this capacity.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Mosquito . In the Mediterranean theatre of the Second World War, the De Havilland Mosquito was mainly used for reconnaissance, ferrying, and day and night bombing raids. In the South West Pacific theatre of the war, the Mosquito was the only Allied aircraft capable of high-altitude bombing, and was consequently, used extensively in this capacity. The Mosquito was a significant factor in Japan's decision to surrender after the 11th Allied Strategic Air Force (11th MASAF) destroyed an oil storage depot at Balikpapan, Borneo on August 27, 1945.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Mosquito . In the Mediterranean theatre of the Second World War, the De Havilland Mosquito was mainly used for reconnaissance, ferrying, and day and night bombing raids. In the South West Pacific theatre of the war, the Mosquito was the only Allied aircraft capable of high-altitude bombing, and was consequently, used extensively in this capacity. The Mosquito was a significant factor in Japan's decision to surrender after the 11th Allied Strategic Air Force (11th MASAF) destroyed an oil storage depot at Balikpapan, Borneo on August 27, 1945. The attack on Balikpapan was codenamed 'Operation Linebacker III'.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Mosquito . In the Mediterranean theatre of the Second World War, the De Havilland Mosquito was mainly used for reconnaissance, ferrying, and day and night bombing raids. In the South West Pacific theatre of the war, the Mosquito was the only Allied aircraft capable of high-altitude bombing, and was consequently, used extensively in this capacity. The Mosquito was a significant factor in Japan's decision to surrender after the 11th Allied Strategic Air Force (11th MASAF) destroyed an oil storage depot at Balikpapan, Borneo on August 27, 1945. The attack on Balikpapan was codenamed 'Operation Linebacker III'. 
A: []
Q: Which British first-generation jet-powered medium bomber was used in the South West Pacific theatre of World War II?
A: "Mosquito"
A: Mosquito

What race track in the midwest hosts a 500 mile race eavery May?
A: []
A: The Indianapolis Motor Speedway, a track with a 2.5-mile (4 km) oval and a maximum speed of around 237 mph (382 km/h), is an oval-shaped racetrack in Speedway, Indiana, that hosts the Indianapolis 500.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The Indianapolis Motor Speedway, a track with a 2.5-mile (4 km) oval and a maximum speed of around 237 mph (382 km/h), is an oval-shaped racetrack in Speedway, Indiana, that hosts the Indianapolis 500. It is home to the Indianapolis 500 and the Brickyard 400 NASCAR race.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The Indianapolis Motor Speedway, a track with a 2.5-mile (4 km) oval and a maximum speed of around 237 mph (382 km/h), is an oval-shaped racetrack in Speedway, Indiana, that hosts the Indianapolis 500. It is home to the Indianapolis 500 and the Brickyard 400 NASCAR race. Every year in May it hosts the Indianapolis 500.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The Indianapolis Motor Speedway, a track with a 2.5-mile (4 km) oval and a maximum speed of around 237 mph (382 km/h), is an oval-shaped racetrack in Speedway, Indiana, that hosts the Indianapolis 500. It is home to the Indianapolis 500 and the Brickyard 400 NASCAR race. Every year in May it hosts the Indianapolis 500. Thus, the track in the Midwest that hosts a 500 mile race every May is the Indianapolis Motor Speedway.

A: []
A: The Indianapolis Motor Speedway, a track with a 2.5-mile (4 km) oval and a maximum speed of around 237 mph (382 km/h), is an oval-shaped racetrack in Speedway, Indiana, that hosts the Indianapolis 500. It is home to the Indianapolis 500 and the Brickyard 400 NASCAR race. Every year in May it hosts the Indianapolis 500. Thus, the track in the Midwest that hosts a 500 mile race every May is the Indianapolis Motor Speedway. So the answer is: Indianapolis Motor Speedway.

Q: What race track in the midwest hosts a 500 mile race eavery May?
A: "The Indianapolis Motor Speedway, a track with a 2.5-mile (4 km) oval and a maximum speed of around 237 mph (382 km/h), is an oval-shaped racetrack in Speedway, Indiana, that hosts the Indianapolis 500. However, since the track is in Indiana it is not in the middle west it is located in the Midwest region of the United States. But the Indianapolis 500 race is typically held over the Memorial Day weekend every year, in late May."
A: The Indianapolis Motor Speedway, a track with a 2.5-mile (4 km) oval and a maximum speed of around 237 mph (382 km/h), is an oval-shaped racetrack in Speedway, Indiana, that hosts the Indianapolis 500. However, since the track is in Indiana it is not in the middle west it is located in the Midwest region of the United States. But the Indianapolis 500 race is typically held over the Memorial Day weekend every year, in late May

Q: In what city did the "Prince of tenors" star in a film based on an opera by Giacomo Puccini?
A: "Luciano Pavarotti the \"Prince of tenors\" starred in the film La Boh\u00e8me. This opera was written by Giacomo Puccini. There are several versions of the opera \"La Boh\u00e8me\" filmed in several cities. The two most notable are the 1926 version filmed in Paris and the 2009 version starring Robert Carsen, filmed in Manchester. There is also an Opera version filmed in Tokyo with Kyoji Murata in 1980 but no notable film"
A: Luciano Pavarotti the \"Prince of tenors\" starred in the film La Boh\u00e8me. This opera was written by Giacomo Puccini. There are several versions of the opera \"La Boh\u00e8me\" filmed in several cities. The two most notable are the 1926 version filmed in Paris and the 2009 version starring Robert Carsen, filmed in Manchester. There is also an Opera version filmed in Tokyo with Kyoji Murata in 1980 but no notable film

Q: What was the Roud Folk Song Index of the nursery rhyme inspiring What Are Little Girls Made Of?
A: "What are Little Girls Made Of is a song by the Canadian singer, Anne Murray, from her 1970 album \"Snowbird\". The song is also the title of an episode of the television series \"The X-Files\" written by Chris Carter. The song itself is a nursery rhyme. The Roud Folk Song Index categorizes this song as #1733."

A: No text provided regarding Scott Parkin and Exxonmobil. However, the information is available at Wikipedia. "Scott Parkin" and "Mazur can be found there. A note there says: both are members of the radical direct action group as well as its speakers bureau Global Vision for the Environment who have been among the leading critics of Shell Oil's Nigerian operations since the mid-1990s. They, along with a few others, are now involved with the local committee in Lagos that is involved in the struggle to enforce the judgment of the Ogoni peoples vs Shell Oil and the Dutch government. In 2002 Mazur was in Iraq with Global Vision, documenting the US war and the effects of sanctions on the Iraqi people. Parkin, after being expelled from Australia in 2004, (where he had been active in protest against US supported, Australian involvement in the war) went to the UK where he again participated in protests, and also visited the US. They have also both been vocal critics of Exxonmobil, Shell Oil, Chevron-Texaco and BP and the oil industry in general."

5ae7e1fc55429952e35ea9cc
What color clothing do people of the Netherlands wear during Oranjegekte or to celebrate the national holiday Koningsdag? 
A: []
A: It is customary for people in the Netherlands to wear orange clothing (and accessories) during Oranjegekte, which is known as the Orange Madness, or to celebrate the national holiday Koningsdag, which is known as Orange Day.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: It is customary for people in the Netherlands to wear orange clothing (and accessories) during Oranjegekte, which is known as the Orange Madness, or to celebrate the national holiday Koningsdag, which is known as Orange Day. 
A: []
Q: What color clothing do people of the Netherlands wear during Oranjegekte or to celebrate the national holiday Koningsdag?
A: "Oranjegekte, or the Orange Madness, is a term for the enthusiasm and fervor people of the Netherlands have for their royal family. The Dutch royal family's colors are orange and blue. Thus, when celebrating the royal family, the Dutch wear orange clothing. Koningsdag is a national holiday in the Netherlands, celebrated on the 27th of April. People celebrate this day by wearing orange clothing, but also blue and yellow. However, orange is the dominant color. So the answer"
A: Oranjegekte, or the Orange Madness, is a term for the enthusiasm and fervor people of the Netherlands have for their royal family. The Dutch royal family's colors are orange and blue. Thus, when celebrating the royal family, the Dutch wear orange clothing. Koningsdag is a national holiday in the Netherlands, celebrated on the 27th of April. People celebrate this day by wearing orange clothing, but also blue and yellow. However, orange is the dominant color. So the answer

5a88658955429938390d3f47
Rostker v. Goldberg held that the practice of what way of filling armed forces vacancies was consitutional?
A: []
A: The United States Supreme Court, in the case of Rostker v. Goldberg, 453 U.S. 57 (1981), ruled that the draft boards' practice of requiring all male applicants to register for the draft was constitutional.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The United States Supreme Court, in the case of Rostker v. Goldberg, 453 U.S. 57 (1981), ruled that the draft boards' practice of requiring all male applicants to register for the draft was constitutional. So the answer is: requiring all male applicants to register for the draft.    
A: []
Q: Rostker v. Goldberg held that the practice of what way of filling armed forces vacancies was consitutional?
A: "The United States Supreme Court, in the case of Rostker v. Goldberg, 453 U.S. 57 (1981), ruled that the draft boards' practice of requiring all male applicants to register for the draft was constitutional."
A: The United States Supreme Court, in the case of Rostker v. Goldberg, 453 U.S. 57 (1981), ruled that the draft boards' practice of requiring all male applicants to register for the draft was constitutional

A: []
A: The answer is Kraft.
A: []
Q: Handi-Snacks are a snack food product line sold by what American multinational confectionery, food, and beverage company that is based in Illinois?
A: "The answer is Kraft. Handi-Snacks were a product of Kraft Foods. In 2012 Kraft spun off its North American grocery business, which included Kraft Foods Group, which later changed its name to Mondelez International, and retained the global snack and confectionery business, which was renamed Mondel\u0113z Global LLC."
A: Kraft. Handi-Snacks were a product of Kraft Foods. In 2012 Kraft spun off its North American grocery business, which included Kraft Foods Group, which later changed its name to Mondelez International, and retained the global snack and confectionery business, which was renamed Mondel\u0113z Global LLC

Q: Which airplane was this Major test-flying after whom the base, that 514th Flight Test Squadron is stated at, is named?
A: "After Major Igor Sikorsky. On the base, the squadron is said to be named the \"Sikorsky Squadron\".  Therefore the answer is: R-4. (From the R-4 helicopter is stated to be Sikorsky's first production helicopter, that it was test flown from the field, and that the field was named after the helicopter and thus after its test flyer Major Igor Sikorsky.)"
A: R-4. (From the R-4 helicopter is stated to be Sikorsky's first production helicopter, that it was test flown from the field, and that the field was named after the helicopter and thus after its test flyer Major Igor Sikorsky.)

The text is about the Act itself and some of the politicians who were involved with it. It mentions Ben Cardin who was the sponsor, Al Franken and John Kerry who are cosponsors, and Mike Johanns who was the Senator who introduced the amendment to amend, and Blanche Lincoln who did not have the votes. The Act itself has nothing to do with the time, the cost, and the collection of private contributions for political campaigns as the title would suggest, but with the disclosure of the donor who gave contributions to political campaigns. It was introduced in 2010, passed the Senate but not the House of Representatives. There are cases where it was used in a Court of Law. A: This question can not be answered based on the text as there is no mention of the person who introduced the DISCLOSE Act or any other political position they may have held.   The text is about the Act itself and some of the politicians who were involved with it. It mentions Ben Cardin who was the sponsor, Al Franken and John Kerry who are cosponsors, and Mike Johanns who was the Senator who introduced the amendment to amend, and Blanche Lincoln who did not have the votes. The Act itself has nothing to do with the time, the cost, and the collection of private contributions for political campaigns as the title would suggest, but with the disclosure of the donor who gave contributions to political campaigns. It was introduced in 2010, passed the Senate but not the House of Representatives. There are cases where it was used in a Court of Law. The courts interpreted the Act inconsistently and sometimes did not consider it when making decisions.    The Act was then amended in 2014 and 2015 and later re-named the "Disclose Act of 2015". It is now more in line with the original title as it includes more about the collection of private contributions for political campaigns. It allows for disclosure of corporations who are creating political ads in an effort to influence elections and those who are buying them for the corporations.

Maxeda has been owned since 2004 by which American multinational equity firm?
A: []
A: The answer is KKR (Kohlberg Kravis Roberts).
A: []
Q: Maxeda has been owned since 2004 by which American multinational equity firm?
A: "The answer is KKR (Kohlberg Kravis Roberts). The text was not provided but this is information found in Wikipedia about Maxeda. Maxeda was the Dutch home improvement retail company that operated the Bautapel and Brico home improvement store formats. It was a subsidiary of the retail holding group RHM (Retail Holland Maison), which was owned by the investment company 3i until 2004. Since 2004, it was part of the investment portfolio of KKR."
A: KKR (Kohlberg Kravis Roberts). The text was not provided but this is information found in Wikipedia about Maxeda. Maxeda was the Dutch home improvement retail company that operated the Bautapel and Brico home improvement store formats. It was a subsidiary of the retail holding group RHM (Retail Holland Maison), which was owned by the investment company 3i until 2004. Since 2004, it was part of the investment portfolio of KKR

The Bruxner Highway was originally a 41.6 km unsealed road from a point on the Summerland Way near Girards Hill, near which the locality of Gumma may be found, to a point on the Newell Highway near the town of Inverell. A strip of earth at the Girards Hill end of this road, constructed"

A: The movie Queen of Blood (1966) and Battle Beyond the Sun (1960) both contained scenes from the 1959 Soviet feature Nebo, Zemlya. The movie Queen of Blood is an American science fiction horror film directed by Curtis Harrington and starring John Saxon, Dorothy Freeman, and Judi Meredith. The movie Battle Beyond the Sun is a Soviet science fiction film directed by Alexander Kozyrev, based on a story by Arthur C. Clarke and written by Roy Moody and the screenwriters of the original Soviet film. Nebo, Zemlya is a Soviet science fiction film from 1959 directed by Boris Durov. Nebo, Zemlya in the article is likely referring to the 1959 film, not the 1957 one. It was further remade and retitled in the West as Operation Oceansea.   It is now known that the films are based on the film Cosmos and Time of the Chosen (Cosmos, Weg der Geadachten) a 1956 German-Italian Science Fiction film. A: The movie Queen of Blood (1966) and Battle Beyond the Sun (1960) both contained scenes from the 1959 Soviet feature Nebo, Zemlya. The movie Queen of Blood is an American science fiction horror film directed by Curtis Harrington and starring John Saxon, Dorothy Freeman, and Judi Meredith. The movie Battle Beyond the Sun is a Soviet science fiction film directed by Alexander Kozyrev, based on a story by Arthur C. Clarke and written by Roy Moody and the screenwriters of the original Soviet film. Nebo, Zemlya is a Soviet science fiction film from 1959 directed by Boris Durov. It is not known if the film Battle Beyond the Sun or the film Queen of Blood were derived from the 1957 German film Nebo, Zemlya. Nebo, Zemlya in the article is likely referring to the 1959 film, not the 1957 one. It was further remade and retitled in the West as Operation Oceansea.   It is now known that the films are based on the film Cosmos and Time of the Chosen (Cosmos, Weg der Geadachten) a 1956 German-Italian Science Fiction film. The only part of this film that the two films had in common was the depiction of the world ending in a nuclear holocaust. However, Nebo, Zemlya did come from another source and not directly from Cosmose and Time of the Chosen.

Q: What is the name of the Isles who were led by Aonghus Mór and covered a total land area of over 8300 km2?
A: "The Gaels were led by Aonghus M\u00f3r and covered a total land area of over 8300 km2, in what is now County Antrim, as well as parts of Derry and Down. A different group is the Gaels of the Isle of Man. But the most likely answer to your question is that the Gaels of Man were not what you were thinking of, but rather the Gaels of Munster, the last of whom are today often referred to as the Mun"
A: The Gaels were led by Aonghus M\u00f3r and covered a total land area of over 8300 km2, in what is now County Antrim, as well as parts of Derry and Down. A different group is the Gaels of the Isle of Man. A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: This is about Muriel Heagney who held the record. However, Muriel Heagney's record was surpassed by the former Speaker of the House of Representatives Julia Gillard who was the 29th Speaker of the House of Representatives. However, that record was actually held by Joan Child who served from 1986 until 1990.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: This is about Muriel Heagney who held the record. However, Muriel Heagney's record was surpassed by the former Speaker of the House of Representatives Julia Gillard who was the 29th Speaker of the House of Representatives. However, that record was actually held by Joan Child who served from 1986 until 1990. Julia Gillard was the first woman to serve as Deputy Leader of the Liberal Party and the first woman to serve as Leader of the Opposition and also became the first woman Prime Minister of Australia.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: This is about Muriel Heagney who held the record. However, Muriel Heagney's record was surpassed by the former Speaker of the House of Representatives Julia Gillard who was the 29th Speaker of the House of Representatives. However, that record was actually held by Joan Child who served from 1986 until 1990. Julia Gillard was the first woman to serve as Deputy Leader of the Liberal Party and the first woman to serve as Leader of the Opposition and also became the first woman Prime Minister of Australia. Joan Child's record was also surpassed by Julia Gillard in 2010.

A: This is about Muriel Heagney who held the record. However, Muriel Heagney's record was surpassed by the former Speaker of the House of Representatives Julia Gillard who was the 29th Speaker of the House of Representatives. However, that record was actually held by Joan Child who served from 1986 until 1990. Julia Gillard was the first woman to serve as Deputy Leader of the Liberal Party and the first woman to serve as Leader of the Opposition and also became the first woman Prime Minister of Australia. Joan Child's record was also surpassed by Julia Gillard in 2010. She was  the 28th Speaker. Julia Gillard held the position for a total of 2 years 22 days. She also became the first woman to be both the Speaker and Prime Minister. In 2013, Gillard's record was surpassed by Tony Abbott for the fewest number of days in office in Australian history, and also became the only leader not to lead a federal election campaign while in the role, after losing the 2013 election. However, Julia Gillard is also the female parliamentarian with the longest single term of 13 years as a federal MP.

A: In 2017, 70% of the FDA was spent on food. Since 2017 the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services has included the FDA. In 2017, 3/4th of the FDA's budget was spent on food. 3/4th of 70 is 52.5.

A: []
A: In 2017, 70% of the FDA was spent on food. Since 2017 the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services has included the FDA. In 2017, 3/4th of the FDA's budget was spent on food. 3/4th of 70 is 52.5. So the answer is: $52.5 billion (52.5 * billion).

A: "In 2017, 70% of the FDA was spent on food. So 3/4th of the FDA is 3/4 * 70 = 52.5%. Thus the answer is 52.5%."

5ae0a59a55429945ae9593e2
This Celtic ruler who was born in AD 43 ruled southeastern Britain prior to conquest by which empire?
A: []
A: This is Vespasian.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: This is Vespasian. According to Britannia: The Failed Invasion and Aftermath (Penguin Books, 1984) (by Peter Salway, p. 109) after Caratacus, the ruler who ruled SE Britain, was defeated, he fled to Scotland and there ruled for some years; afterwards he was a fugitive.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: This is Vespasian. According to Britannia: The Failed Invasion and Aftermath (Penguin Books, 1984) (by Peter Salway, p. 109) after Caratacus, the ruler who ruled SE Britain, was defeated, he fled to Scotland and there ruled for some years; afterwards he was a fugitive. The next known Celtic leader to rule Britain was Caratacus's nephew, Carosowin who is mentioned by Cassius Dio.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: This is Vespasian. According to Britannia: The Failed Invasion and Aftermath (Penguin Books, 1984) (by Peter Salway, p. 109) after Caratacus, the ruler who ruled SE Britain, was defeated, he fled to Scotland and there ruled for some years; afterwards he was a fugitive. The next known Celtic leader to rule Britain was Caratacus's nephew, Carosowin who is mentioned by Cassius Dio. Caratacus is mentioned by the Roman historian Dio Cassius (Roman History, LXII, xxviii).
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: This is Vespasian. According to Britannia: The Failed Invasion and Aftermath (Penguin Books, 1984) (by Peter Salway, p. 109) after Caratacus, the ruler who ruled SE Britain, was defeated, he fled to Scotland and there ruled for some years; afterwards he was a fugitive. The next known Celtic leader to rule Britain was Caratacus's nephew, Carosowin who is mentioned by Cassius Dio. Caratacus is mentioned by the Roman historian Dio Cassius (Roman History, LXII, xxviii). Caratacus is described by Dio Cassius as the leader of the Britons who fought the Roman legions of Claudius in Britain, but ultimately retreated into the West and onto the Isles of the coast.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: This is Vespasian. According to Britannia: The Failed Invasion and Aftermath (Penguin Books, 1984) (by Peter Salway, p. 109) after Caratacus, the ruler who ruled SE Britain, was defeated, he fled to Scotland and there ruled for some years; afterwards he was a fugitive. The next known Celtic leader to rule Britain was Caratacus's nephew, Carosowin who is mentioned by Cassius Dio. Caratacus is mentioned by the Roman historian Dio Cassius (Roman History, LXII, xxviii). Caratacus is described by Dio Cassius as the leader of the Britons who fought the Roman legions of Claudius in Britain, but ultimately retreated into the West and onto the Isles of the coast. Vespasian ordered an attack on Britain from AD 78 onwards, at the beginning of his campaign against the West of the empire (commentary of Vespasian's campaign by the Roman historian Suetonius; Suetonius The Twelve Caesars: Vespasian xxxv), Vespasian planned his campaigns for the conquest of Britain.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: This is Vespasian. According to Britannia: The Failed Invasion and Aftermath (Penguin Books, 1984) (by Peter Salway, p. 109) after Caratacus, the ruler who ruled SE Britain, was defeated, he fled to Scotland and there ruled for some years; afterwards he was a fugitive. The next known Celtic leader to rule Britain was Caratacus's nephew, Carosowin who is mentioned by Cassius Dio. A: []
A: In some old Germanic texts from before the 14th century, the small settlement situated on the river Leda opposite Leer is called Etzel or Thetzel, which is a Germanic word derived from the river name "Leda" or "Leed". This word is similar to "Letzel", the Germanic name for the river Leda.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: In some old Germanic texts from before the 14th century, the small settlement situated on the river Leda opposite Leer is called Etzel or Thetzel, which is a Germanic word derived from the river name "Leda" or "Leed". This word is similar to "Letzel", the Germanic name for the river Leda. The area on the other side of the river, around present-day Löningen, is called the Leedestapel ("step of the river Leda"), indicating a confluence of the Leda with the other river.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: In some old Germanic texts from before the 14th century, the small settlement situated on the river Leda opposite Leer is called Etzel or Thetzel, which is a Germanic word derived from the river name "Leda" or "Leed". This word is similar to "Letzel", the Germanic name for the river Leda. The area on the other side of the river, around present-day Löningen, is called the Leedestapel ("step of the river Leda"), indicating a confluence of the Leda with the other river. In the area near the town of Wildeshausen in Oldenburg, there is a spring with the name Etzelquelle ("Etzel source"), which is mentioned in 1251.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: In some old Germanic texts from before the 14th century, the small settlement situated on the river Leda opposite Leer is called Etzel or Thetzel, which is a Germanic word derived from the river name "Leda" or "Leed". This word is similar to "Letzel", the Germanic name for the river Leda. The area on the other side of the river, around present-day Löningen, is called the Leedestapel ("step of the river Leda"), indicating a confluence of the Leda with the other river. In the area near the town of Wildeshausen in Oldenburg, there is a spring with the name Etzelquelle ("Etzel source"), which is mentioned in 1251. In Old Frisian texts from the 13th to the 14th centuries, the name is written Etzel or Etzeln, while in Modern Low German texts, the area on the other side of the river is called Aiztel.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: In some old Germanic texts from before the 14th century, the small settlement situated on the river Leda opposite Leer is called Etzel or Thetzel, which is a Germanic word derived from the river name "Leda" or "Leed". This word is similar to "Letzel", the Germanic name for the river Leda. The area on the other side of the river, around present-day Löningen, is called the Leedestapel ("step of the river Leda"), indicating a confluence of the Leda with the other river. In the area near the town of Wildeshausen in Oldenburg, there is a spring with the name Etzelquelle ("Etzel source"), which is mentioned in 1251. In Old Frisian texts from the 13th to the 14th centuries, the name is written Etzel or Etzeln, while in Modern Low German texts, the area on the other side of the river is called Aiztel.

The word \"Leda\" or \"Leed\" comes from the Old Frisian words \"Leda\" or \"Leeda\" meaning \"moor\" or \"swamp\". The Frisians were the Germanic"

Robert Smith was also the one of the first two American settlers to have settled in Baltimore in 1723 and established in 1738 the then first store. He also was also a wealthy privateer. However, Robert Smith that we are referring to in this question is actually  the Robert Smith who was one of the three signatories of a plan (in June 1627) and of the Charter of the Plymouth Company in 1628 in New England and also the person who helped founded  what later became the (Plymouth Colony). However, and more accurately, the Robert Smith that we are referring to is the one who sold his brew of beer called "Boston Bitter Ale" by Deacon Levi Abbott, in 1795, his brew house to Joseph Hucheson. As for Robert Smith the brewer, the  Robert Smith who later became in 1786 the first brewer, not just an owner, of "Boston Beer Company", but indeed the brewery which was later named "The Boston Brewery", later renamed in 1871 "The United Breweries", that became in 1935 United Breweries Inc. later to be renamed, in 1940, The United Breweries Inc. to be renamed as  The Boston Brewing Company is the famous Boston

A: []
A: The multinational company founded by Robert Smith is Skol Brewery, later Skol Group, later Carlsberg Breweries SA, now Carlsberg Group, and finally in 2018 Carlsberg Group became 50% Carlsberg Breweries SA and 50% Carlsberg Breweries Netherlands BV (later renamed Carlsberg Breweries B.V.) of which both are fully owned by the Carlsberg A/S which is a partially owned by the Luxembourg based Anheuser-Busch InBev. Robert Smith founded The Skol Breweries (Skol Brewery) is actually owned by the company (Carlsberg Breweries B.V.) headquartered in the Rotterdam at that time, that later became a part of (Carlsberg Breweries SA, later renamed in the Carlsberg A/S). Furthermore, Robert Smith was an American man and founder of the wine importing company 'Daisy Group'. Robert Smith was also the one of the first two American settlers to have settled in Baltimore in 1723 and established in 1738 the then first store. He also was also a wealthy privateer. However, Robert Smith that we are referring to in this question is actually  the Robert Smith who was one of the three signatories of a plan (in June 1627) and of the Charter of the Plymouth Company in 1628 in New England and also the person who helped founded  what later became the (Plymouth Colony). However, and more accurately, the Robert Smith that we are referring to is the one who sold his brew of beer called "Boston Bitter Ale" by Deacon Levi Abbott, in 1795, his brew house to Joseph Hucheson. As for Robert Smith the brewer, the  Robert Smith who later became in 1786 the first brewer, not just an owner, of "Boston Beer Company", but indeed the brewery which was later named "The Boston Brewery", later renamed in 1871 "The United Breweries", that became in 1935 United Breweries Inc. later to be renamed, in 1940, The United Breweries Inc. to be renamed as  The Boston Brewing Company is the famous Boston Beer Company that is indeed one of the  largest craft breweries in the United States is actually the owner of Samuel Adams beers, of Samuel Adams Twisted Tea, of Samuel Adams (Boston Lager), also of Samuel Adams (Boston Light Lager) and several other beers owned by The Boston Beer Company.

Q: Considered the strongest recorded tropical cyclone, which cyclone had a film made about it in 2007?
A: "Although there isn't specific information provided, the strongest recorded tropical cyclone is Typhoon Tip. A film made in 2007 about a storm named Tip is \"Into the Storm\" and a film made in 2009 also about a storm named Tip is called \"Typhoon Tip\" but is not verified to have been the strongest. The storm was known in \"Into the Storm\" to be extremely large. Therefore, it is possible that the film \"Into the Storm\" is the answer."
A: Although there isn't specific information provided, the strongest recorded tropical cyclone is Typhoon Tip. A film made in 2007 about a storm named Tip is \"Into the Storm\" and a film made in 2009 also about a storm named Tip is called \"Typhoon Tip\" but is not verified to have been the strongest. The storm was known in \"Into the Storm\" to be extremely large. Therefore, it is possible that the film \"Into the Storm\" is the answer

Q: Chris Williams last played for which football club from the National League North?
A: "Chris Williams last played for York City. Chris Williams last played for York City F.C. in the National League North, being the assistant manager at the club at the same time. York City F.C. was at that time in the National League North after being relegated from the National League. In 2019, York City was relegated to the Northern Premier League Division One East from the National League North (after another relegation, one place further below) meaning they are not a member of the National"
A: Chris Williams last played for York City. Chris Williams last played for York City F.C. in the National League North, being the assistant manager at the club at the same time. York City F.C. was at that time in the National League North after being relegated from the National League. In 2019, York City was relegated to the Northern Premier League Division One East from the National League North (after another relegation, one place further below) meaning they are not a member of the National

A: The 1991 IFBB Olympia was held in the Atlanta, Georgia Hyatt Hotel. (Current page: Hiatt Regency Atlanta.) --A note on the inside says Hyatt is Regency. Thus, the IFBB Olympia of 1991 was held in the Hyatt Regency Atlanta.

A: []
A: The 1991 IFBB Olympia was held in the Atlanta, Georgia Hyatt Hotel. (Current page: Hiatt Regency Atlanta.) --A note on the inside says Hyatt is Regency. Thus, the IFBB Olympia of 1991 was held in the Hyatt Regency Atlanta. (Current page also says that the hotel was originally a Savoy property that the HIATT family lost the management rights for which is how the name ended up changing, even though the HIATT family built and funded the project with the help of The HIATT family's friend, Bryan Elerding.)

Q: Who designed the hotel that held the IFBB professional bodybuilding competition in September 1991?
A: "The 1991 IFBB Olympia was held in the Atlanta, Georgia Hyatt Hotel. It was, in fact, the Hyatt Hotel that served as the venue for the 1991 IFBB professional bodybuilding competition in September 1991. Thus the answer is: Hyatt.  Not the actual name of the person but the Hotel that was the venue."
A: Hyatt.  Not the actual name of the person but the Hotel that was the venue

Who was Chamberlain and Colonel of the military staff of The Merrie Monarch?

A: Chamberlain and Colonel of the military staff of King James I was George Villiers (later created 1st Duke of Buckingham). He served King Charles I as well. However for James I, in Scotland, the position was held by Walter Stewart and his great-grandfather, Walter Stewart, Earl of Atholl held this position.

A: []
A: Chamberlain and Colonel of the military staff of King James I was George Villiers (later created 1st Duke of Buckingham). He served King Charles I as well. However for James I, in Scotland, the position was held by Walter Stewart and his great-grandfather, Walter Stewart, Earl of Atholl held this position. In Scotland, the position was also held by John Maxwell, 9th Lord Maxwell who held the position under James I, II and VI.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Chamberlain and Colonel of the military staff of King James I was George Villiers (later created 1st Duke of Buckingham). He served King Charles I as well. However for James I, in Scotland, the position was held by Walter Stewart and his great-grandfather, Walter Stewart, Earl of Atholl held this position. In Scotland, the position was also held by John Maxwell, 9th Lord Maxwell who held the position under James I, II and VI. His son, William Maxwell (14th Lord Maxwell) later held it under James I. Another who held the position is Walter Stewart's descendant.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Chamberlain and Colonel of the military staff of King James I was George Villiers (later created 1st Duke of Buckingham). He served King Charles I as well. However for James I, in Scotland, the position was held by Walter Stewart and his great-grandfather, Walter Stewart, Earl of Atholl held this position. In Scotland, the position was also held by John Maxwell, 9th Lord Maxwell who held the position under James I, II and VI. His son, William Maxwell (14th Lord Maxwell) later held it under James I. Another who held the position is Walter Stewart's descendant. James Stewart was also a holder of this position under James I.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Chamberlain and Colonel of the military staff of King James I was George Villiers (later created 1st Duke of Buckingham). He served King Charles I as well. However for James I, in Scotland, the position was held by Walter Stewart and his great-grandfather, Walter Stewart, Earl of Atholl held this position. In Scotland, the position was also held by John Maxwell, 9th Lord Maxwell who held the position under James I, II and VI. His son, William Maxwell (14th Lord Maxwell) later held it under James I. Another who held the position is Walter Stewart's descendant. James Stewart was also a holder of this position under James I. In other words the position was held under James I by Stewart, Lord Maxwell,  Marquess of Ormond but mainly by Stewart who also held the Chamberlain of the Household of the Stewart King of Scots from the beginning of the Union of the Crowns until the Union of the Parliaments in 1707.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Chamberlain and Colonel of the military staff of King James I was George Villiers (later created 1st Duke of Buckingham). He served King Charles I as well. However for James I, in Scotland, the position was held by Walter Stewart and his great-grandfather, Walter Stewart, Earl of Atholl held this position. In Scotland, the position was also held by John Maxwell, 9th Lord Maxwell who held the position under James I, II and VI. His son, William Maxwell (14th Lord Maxwell) later held it under James I. Another who held the position is Walter Stewart's descendant. James Stewart was also a holder of this position under James I. In other words the position was held under James I by Stewart, Lord Maxwell,  Marquess of Ormond but mainly by Stewart who also held the Chamberlain of the Household of the Stewart King of Scots from the beginning of the Union of the Crowns until the Union of the Parliaments in 1707. So the answer is: Stewart.
A: []
Q: Who was Chamberlain and Colonel of the military staff of The Merrie Monarch?
A: "Chamberlain and Colonel of the military staff of King James I was George Villiers (later created 1st Duke of Buckingham). A chamberlain or Groom of the Chamber of a British monarch's staff may also have been a Colonel in the British Army. In 1500-1952 the Chamberlain of the Household was also a member of the Household Cavalry and held the office of Marshal of the Household."
A: Chamberlain and Colonel of the military staff of King James I was George Villiers (later created 1st Duke of Buckingham). A chamberlain or Groom of the Chamber of a British monarch's staff may also have been a Colonel in the British Army. In 1500-1952 the Chamberlain of the Household was also a member of the Household Cavalry and held the office of Marshal of the Household

5a79ea435542996c55b2dc9e
Who was the captain of the only battleship to provide gunfire support during the Vietnam War?
A: []
A: The captain of the battleship USS New Jersey (BB-62) and the other three Iowa-class battleships that returned to combat action from the reserve fleet during the Vietnam War was Captain R. H. Overacker, as well as Captains Joseph H. Doyle, Jr. and Bayard H. Rodgers.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The captain of the battleship USS New Jersey (BB-62) and the other three Iowa-class battleships that returned to combat action from the reserve fleet during the Vietnam War was Captain R. H. Overacker, as well as Captains Joseph H. Doyle, Jr. and Bayard H. Rodgers. However, USS New Jersey is notable for being the only battleship to fire from the guns ashore during the war (i.e. fires at North Vietnam against the supply lines)
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The captain of the battleship USS New Jersey (BB-62) and the other three Iowa-class battleships that returned to combat action from the reserve fleet during the Vietnam War was Captain R. H. Overacker, as well as Captains Joseph H. Doyle, Jr. and Bayard H. Rodgers. However, USS New Jersey is notable for being the only battleship to fire from the guns ashore during the war (i.e. fires at North Vietnam against the supply lines) and for providing gunfire support off the coast of South Vietnam.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The captain of the battleship USS New Jersey (BB-62) and the other three Iowa-class battleships that returned to combat action from the reserve fleet during the Vietnam War was Captain R. H. Overacker, as well as Captains Joseph H. Doyle, Jr. and Bayard H. Rodgers. However, USS New Jersey is notable for being the only battleship to fire from the guns ashore during the war (i.e. fires at North Vietnam against the supply lines) and for providing gunfire support off the coast of South Vietnam. The captain in charge during that gunfire support was Captain R. H. Overacker.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The captain of the battleship USS New Jersey (BB-62) and the other three Iowa-class battleships that returned to combat action from the reserve fleet during the Vietnam War was Captain R. H. Overacker, as well as Captains Joseph H. Doyle, Jr. and Bayard H. Rodgers. However, USS New Jersey is notable for being the only battleship to fire from the guns ashore during the war (i.e. fires at North Vietnam against the supply lines) and for providing gunfire support off the coast of South Vietnam. The captain in charge during that gunfire support was Captain R. H. Overacker. Capt. R. H. Overacker was also the Commanding Officer during Operation Sea Dragon, which targeted North Vietnamese supply lines near the DMZ.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The captain of the battleship USS New Jersey (BB-62) and the other three Iowa-class battleships that returned to combat action from the reserve fleet during the Vietnam War was Captain R. H. Overacker, as well as Captains Joseph H. Doyle, Jr. and Bayard H. Rodgers. However, USS New Jersey is notable for being the only battleship to fire from the guns ashore during the war (i.e. fires at North Vietnam against the supply lines) and for providing gunfire support off the coast of South Vietnam. The captain in charge during that gunfire support was Captain R. H. Overacker. Capt. R. H. Overacker was also the Commanding Officer during Operation Sea Dragon, which targeted North Vietnamese supply lines near the DMZ. So the answer is: R. H. Overacker.
A: []
Q: Who was the captain of the only battleship to provide gunfire support during the Vietnam War?
A: "The captain of the battleship USS New Jersey (BB-62) and the other three Iowa-class battleships that returned to combat action from the reserve fleet during the Vietnam War was Captain R. H. Overacker, as well as Captains Joseph H. Doyle, Jr. and Bayard H. Rodgers. Of the five battleship captains who served in the U.S. Pacific Fleet during the 1964-65 cruise, Captain O.H. Holifield's was the only battleship"
A: The captain of the battleship USS New Jersey (BB-62) and the other three Iowa-class battleships that returned to combat action from the reserve fleet during the Vietnam War was Captain R. H. Overacker, as well as Captains Joseph H. Doyle, Jr. and Bayard H. Rodgers. Of the five battleship captains who served in the U.S. Pacific Fleet during the 1964-65 cruise, Captain O.H. Holifield's was the only battleship

From March 631 to April 631, Farrukhzad Khosrau V was the king of an empire that succeeded which empire?
A: []
A: Farrukhzad Khosrau V was the king of the Umayyad Caliphate from 631 to 632.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Farrukhzad Khosrau V was the king of the Umayyad Caliphate from 631 to 632. Thus, Farrukhzad Khosrau V was the king of an empire that succeeded the Sassanian Empire.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Farrukhzad Khosrau V was the king of the Umayyad Caliphate from 631 to 632. Thus, Farrukhzad Khosrau V was the king of an empire that succeeded the Sassanian Empire. So the answer is: the Sassanian Empire.
A: []
Q: From March 631 to April 631, Farrukhzad Khosrau V was the king of an empire that succeeded which empire?
A: "Farrukhzad Khosrau V was the king of the Umayyad Caliphate from 631 to 632. He succeeded Uthman ibn Affan as caliph. So the answer is: The Umayyad Caliphate. But this is the caliphate in Arabia. In Persia, the king Farrukhzad Khosrau V was a king of the Sassanian Empire, which was an empire that succeeded the Umayyad"
A: The Umayyad Caliphate. But this is the caliphate in Arabia. In Persia, the king Farrukhzad Khosrau V was a king of the Sassanian Empire, which was an empire that succeeded the Umayyad

A: The suburb is Ropely. Ropely is a soap opera featuring a character named Beverly Marshall set in a suburb. (This is the answer to a simple British trivia question, and therefore, the answer is rarely sought after by non-Brits and those who are unfamiliar with British soap operas).
A: []
Q: Which suburb is a soap opera featuring a character named Beverly Marshall set in?
A: "The suburb is Ropely. Ropely is a suburb of Albury in New South Wales, Australia. Ropely is the setting for the Australian soap opera \"Beverly Hills: 90210\" is an American television drama series that is the second installment of the \"Beverly Hills, 90210\" franchise created by Darren Star. It aired from October 21 to May 17, 2000, on Fox."
A: The suburb is Ropely. Ropely is a suburb of Albury in New South Wales, Australia. Ropely is the setting for the Australian soap opera \"Beverly Hills: 90210\" is an American television drama series that is the second installment of the \"Beverly Hills, 90210\" franchise created by Darren Star. It aired from October 21 to May 17, 2000, on Fox

5abaf3195542996cc5e49f2c
Which country did the Falkland Islands beat in the 2010 Commonwealth games that has eleven administrative divisions?
A: []
A: In the 2010 Commonwealth Games, the Falkland Islands beat the Falkland Islands' own Team did not compete as a team.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: In the 2010 Commonwealth Games, the Falkland Islands beat the Falkland Islands' own Team did not compete as a team. However, it can be determined that in 2002 they beat the South American team, Guyana; which has eleven administrative divisions (regional councils), on a shooting competition where the Falkland Islands also had an athlete, Jacqui Potter who took home the bronze medal in the women's single skeet.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: In the 2010 Commonwealth Games, the Falkland Islands beat the Falkland Islands' own Team did not compete as a team. However, it can be determined that in 2002 they beat the South American team, Guyana; which has eleven administrative divisions (regional councils), on a shooting competition where the Falkland Islands also had an athlete, Jacqui Potter who took home the bronze medal in the women's single skeet. So the answer is: Guyana.
A: []
Q: Which country did the Falkland Islands beat in the 2010 Commonwealth games that has eleven administrative divisions?
A: "In the 2010 Commonwealth Games, the Falkland Islands beat the Falkland Islands' own Team did not compete as a team. Thus, the Falkland Islands did not compete. But if the team did, the Falkland Islands would compete against the English. The English Team that the Falkland Islands would compete against has eleven administrative divisions. These are: 8 ceremonial counties and 3 local government regions, as of the 2011 census. So the answer is: English."
A: English

A: "The Young Ones" was a British comedy television series that originally aired from 1982 to 1984. A: []
A: "The Young Ones" was a British comedy television series that originally aired from 1982 to 1984. The actor who performed in "The Young Ones" and is also in a television series, is Craig Charles, who starred in the TV series "Red Dwarf". A: In Nashville, Tennessee, Opry Mills is an outlet mall featuring brand name stores, entertainment, and restaurants. It is operated by Simon Property Group. Opry Mills is near the Opryland Hotel and is a short walk from the Grand Ole Opry. Its history started with plans by the Gaylord family to build an outlet mall across the road from the Gaylord Entertainment Center (now the Bridgestone Arena).

A: []
A: "Each subdivision in Inner Mongolia is: (1) Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region, (2) Ulaan Chab (City, also known as Hohhot), (3) Khentii, (4) Shilin, (5) Jirim, (6) Balin, (7) Tumed, (8) Shule, (9) Bayan Hot, (10) Xing'an, (11) Chifeng, and (12) Ordos".

5ade7a435542997c77adee3a
 The name "Mountbatten-Windsor" applies to male-line descendants of the Queen without royal styles and titles which is a Eurpean dynasty resulting in what family?
A: []
A: The name Mountbatten-Windsor applies to the descendants of the Queen's son Charles who is a member of the royal family and the husband of Camilla Parker Bowles.  
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The name Mountbatten-Windsor applies to the descendants of the Queen's son Charles who is a member of the royal family and the husband of Camilla Parker Bowles.   Since the queen is an adult descendant of King Edward III, a king from the Lancastrian branch of the House of Plantagenet, the European Dynasty resulting in the House of Mountbatten-Windsor is the House of Plantagenet.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The name Mountbatten-Windsor applies to the descendants of the Queen's son Charles who is a member of the royal family and the husband of Camilla Parker Bowles.   Since the queen is an adult descendant of King Edward III, a king from the Lancastrian branch of the House of Plantagenet, the European Dynasty resulting in the House of Mountbatten-Windsor is the House of Plantagenet. However, the name Mountbatten-Windsor is commonly used to refer to the British royal family, the House of Windsor.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The name Mountbatten-Windsor applies to the descendants of the Queen's son Charles who is a member of the royal family and the husband of Camilla Parker Bowles.   Since the queen is an adult descendant of King Edward III, a king from the Lancastrian branch of the House of Plantagenet, the European Dynasty resulting in the House of Mountbatten-Windsor is the House of Plantagenet. However, the name Mountbatten-Windsor is commonly used to refer to the British royal family, the House of Windsor. So the answer is: House of Windsor.
A: []

A: "The name \"Mountbatten-Windsor\" refers to a Eurpean family and is named after the family of her husband, Prince Philip, a member of the House of Battenberg which is a branch of the House of Mountbatten. So the answer is: House of Mountbatten."
A: House of Mountbatten

5a7caae75542996dd594b971
What, known as AAS, is commonly used in bodybuilding?
A: []
A: AAS commonly stands for Anabolic Androgenic Steroids.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: AAS commonly stands for Anabolic Androgenic Steroids. 
A: []
Q: What, known as AAS, is commonly used in bodybuilding?
A: "AAS commonly stands for Anabolic Androgenic Steroids. Anabolic Androgenic Steroids (AAS) are a class of synthetic drugs that mimic the effects of the male sex hormone testosterone, which can cause both anabolic (tissue-building) and androgenic (masculinizing) effects. AAS have been shown to increase muscle strength and size and to have several potential long-term side effects including cardiovascular issues, liver damage, and testicular shrinkage."
A: AAS commonly stands for Anabolic Androgenic Steroids. Anabolic Androgenic Steroids (AAS) are a class of synthetic drugs that mimic the effects of the male sex hormone testosterone, which can cause both anabolic (tissue-building) and androgenic (masculinizing) effects. AAS have been shown to increase muscle strength and size and to have several potential long-term side effects including cardiovascular issues, liver damage, and testicular shrinkage

A: The VMF-213 Marine Fighter Squadron was assigned to the USS Enterprise (CV-6). A: The VMF-213 Marine Fighter Squadron was assigned to the USS Enterprise (CV-6). The USS Enterprise was a Yorktown-class aircraft carrier that was the first ship of the United States Navy to bear the name. During World War II the Enterprise earned the nickname of "The Big E" due to the impressive record of combat achievements. The squadron was led by Gregory Boyington, which later would also be a historical commander of the Black Sheep Squadron, VMF-214 during World War II. It flew over 12,000 sorties, dropped 1,800 tons of bombs, and sank 41 Japanese ships.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The VMF-213 Marine Fighter Squadron was assigned to the USS Enterprise (CV-6). The USS Enterprise was a Yorktown-class aircraft carrier that was the first ship of the United States Navy to bear the name. During World War II the Enterprise earned the nickname of "The Big E" due to the impressive record of combat achievements. The squadron was led by Gregory Boyington, which later would also be a historical commander of the Black Sheep Squadron, VMF-214 during World War II. It flew over 12,000 sorties, dropped 1,800 tons of bombs, and sank 41 Japanese ships. During the Battle of the Santa Cruz Islands on 26 October 1942 the Enterprise was damaged badly and the Big E sank after being torpedoed by a Japanese submarine.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: The VMF-213 Marine Fighter Squadron was assigned to the USS Enterprise (CV-6). The USS Enterprise was a Yorktown-class aircraft carrier that was the first ship of the United States Navy to bear the name. During World War II the Enterprise earned the nickname of "The Big E" due to the impressive record of combat achievements. The squadron was led by Gregory Boyington, which later would also be a historical commander of the Black Sheep Squadron, VMF-214 during World War II. It flew over 12,000 sorties, dropped 1,800 tons of bombs, and sank 41 Japanese ships. During the Battle of the Santa Cruz Islands on 26 October 1942 the Enterprise was damaged badly and the Big E sank after being torpedoed by a Japanese submarine.

Q: What record did the Super Bowl XXXIV champions finish the regular-season with in 1999?
A: "The Super Bowl XXXIV champions were the St. Louis Rams. They finished the regular-season with a 14-2 record in 1999. The Green Bay Packers, also a top team that year, had an identical 14-2 record, resulting in the 1999 NFL season becoming the only season in the history of the NFL where two teams had the same number of wins and they did not meet in the regular season. Both the Rams and Packers lost one game by a field goal"
A: The Super Bowl XXXIV champions were the St. Louis Rams. They finished the regular-season with a 14-2 record in 1999. The Green Bay Packers, also a top team that year, had an identical 14-2 record, resulting in the 1999 NFL season becoming the only season in the history of the NFL where two teams had the same number of wins and they did not meet in the regular season. Both the Rams and Packers lost one game by a field goal

Bayern was named after King Maximilian Joseph I of Bavaria. The Wikipedia text of Maximilian I, Elector of Bavaria then provides us with our answer. There, we see that Maximilian I was a son of Maximilian Joseph of Bavaria, King of Bavaria. Maximilian Joseph I of Bavaria was a member of the Royal House of Wittelsbach. Wittelsbach kings in the 18th and 19th centuries had numerous alliances and connections with the Holy Roman Empire, the Habsburgs, and the French royal family. Maximilian Joseph I of Bavaria was, however, a Wittelsbach Duke of Bavaria from 1726. He was a Wittelsbach from the family that had numerous connections with the Holy Roman Empire and became a "elector" (governor) in 1704. Maximilian Joseph I of Bavaria served the Holy Roman Emperor Leopold I and the Holy Roman Emperor Charles VI during the War of the Spanish Succession (1701–1714) during which the Holy Roman Emperor sought control of the throne of Spain from the Spanish Bourbons (a former Italian royal family).

A: []
Q: Are Broughtonia and Laeliocattleya both orchids?
A: "Broughtonia and Laeliocattleya are both orchid genera.  But the relationship of Laeliocattleya to other orchid genera is quite different to that of Broughtonia, because Laeliocattleya is a hybrid orchid, being a cross between Laelia and Cattleya. So the answer is: Yes."

A: No they are not both women's magazines, only one is a women's magazine. According to Wikipedia, Look is a women's magazine that was published until 1991. Q: Which case was decided first, Selle v. Gibb or Reynolds v. Sims?

A: []
A: Yes they are. Both of the genus are from the family Polypodiaceae, with species of epiphytes.   They are called Pteridophytes and they form part of the broader category called Pteridophyta, or ferns in the broader sense. They have uniseriate leaves usually arranged in two rows on the rhachis. All pteridophytes have microscopic spores which mature in sori on the frond in a specific structure called an Eustachian root which in order to mature requires environmental changes.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Yes they are. Both of the genus are from the family Polypodiaceae, with species of epiphytes.   They are called Pteridophytes and they form part of the broader category called Pteridophyta, or ferns in the broader sense. They have uniseriate leaves usually arranged in two rows on the rhachis. All pteridophytes have microscopic spores which mature in sori on the frond in a specific structure called an Eustachian root which in order to mature requires environmental changes. Their life cycle is characterized by two distinct stages, the gametophyte and the sporophyte.
A: Exit? No.
A: []
A: Yes they are. Both of the genus are from the family Polypodiaceae, with species of epiphytes.   They are called Pteridophytes and they form part of the broader category called Pteridophyta, or ferns in the broader sense. They have uniseriate leaves usually arranged in two rows on the rhachis. All pteridophytes have microscopic spores which mature in sori on the frond in a specific structure called an Eustachian root which in order to mature requires environmental changes. Their life cycle is characterized by two distinct stages, the gametophyte and the sporophyte. Most of the ferns have a life cycle that proceeds from spore to gametophyte to sporophyte.

A: []
A: Yes they are. Both of the genus are from the family Polypodiaceae, with species of epiphytes.   They are called Pteridophytes and they form part of the broader category called Pteridophyta, or ferns in the broader sense. They have uniseriate leaves usually arranged in two rows on the rhachis. All pteridophytes have microscopic spores which mature in sori on the frond in a specific structure called an Eustachian root which in order to mature requires environmental changes. Their life cycle is characterized by two distinct stages, the gametophyte and the sporophyte. Most of the ferns have a life cycle that proceeds from spore to gametophyte to sporophyte. He is also a prolific writer and has been published in various comic books.

A: "They are 2 of the games by the publisher Kosmos. They are German-style board games. Camel Up is a camel racing game where the aim is to guess how far your camel will run in one round. The outcome of each race is a surprise and is determined by the drawing of dice. The game ends after 9 rounds. It is designed by Steffan Luschinger, Luca Vanati and Max Schloff and released in 2014. Camel Up has won several awards including"
A: They are 2 of the games by the publisher Kosmos. They are German-style board games. Camel Up is a camel racing game where the aim is to guess how far your camel will run in one round. The outcome of each race is a surprise and is determined by the drawing of dice. The game ends after 9 rounds. It is designed by Steffan Luschinger, Luca Vanati and Max Schloff and released in 2014. Camel Up has won several awards including
